You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.7 (commit: d47e9f32).

## Issue to Resolve

`SharedFlow.toList(mutableList)`: difference behavior between 1.6.4 vs 1.7.0-Beta
Sometimes, I use this method to write simple test on SharedFlow.
Version 1.6.4 works well.
Since 1.7.0-Beta, this method is deprecated and the implementation seem be wrong

```kotlin
val acc = MutableList()
val job = scope.launch(start = Undispatched) {
  sharedFlow.toList(acc)
}

// later...

job.cancel()
assertContentEquals(
 expected = ...,
 actual = acc
)
```

IMO, the correct impl. is `SharedFlow.toList(acc) = (this as Flow).toList(acc)`

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

+1 to this issue and proposed solution. Seems this commit changed the behavior: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/commit/f981a1d00a9d75169321f5ef63fed16622c3e44f

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

17→class SharedFlowTest : TestBase() {
18→    @Test
19→    fun testRendezvousSharedFlowBasic() = runTest {
20→        expect(1)
21→        val sh = MutableSharedFlow<Int?>()

23→        assertEquals(0, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
24→        sh.emit(1) // no suspend
25→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
26→        assertEquals(0, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
27→        expect(2)
28→        // one collector

30→            expect(3)
31→            sh.collect {
32→                when(it) {
33→                    4 -> expect(5)
34→                    6 -> expect(7)
35→                    10 -> expect(11)
36→                    13 -> expect(14)
37→                    else -> expectUnreached()
38→                }
39→            }
40→            expectUnreached() // does not complete normally
41→        }

43→        assertEquals(1, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
44→        sh.emit(4)
45→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
46→        expect(6)
47→        sh.emit(6)
48→        expect(8)
49→        // one more collector
50→        val job2 = launch(start = CoroutineStart.UNDISPATCHED) {
51→            expect(9)
52→            sh.collect {
53→                when(it) {
54→                    10 -> expect(12)
55→                    13 -> expect(15)
56→                    17 -> expect(18)
57→                    null -> expect(20)
58→                    21 -> expect(22)
59→                    else -> expectUnreached()
60→                }
61→            }
62→            expectUnreached() // does not complete normally
63→        }

66→        sh.emit(10) // to both collectors now!

content.text:
Implemented the missing destination forwarding in the deprecated `SharedFlow` collection terminal ops.

### Fix
In `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/flow/operators/Lint.kt`:
- `SharedFlow.toList(destination)` now delegates to `(this as Flow<T>).toList(destination)`
- `SharedFlow.toSet(destination)` now delegates to `(this as Flow<T>).toSet(destination)`

This restores the 1.6.4-era behavior where the provided mutable destination is populated during collection (even if the collecting coroutine is later cancelled).

### Regression tests
Added a new test file:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/test/flow/sharing/SharedFlowToListDestinationTest.kt`

It verifies that cancelling the collecting job still leaves the provided destination list/set filled with the values emitted so far.

17→class SharedFlowTest : TestBase() {
18→    @Test
19→    fun testRendezvousSharedFlowBasic() = runTest {
20→        expect(1)
21→        val sh = MutableSharedFlow<Int?>()
22→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
23→        assertEquals(0, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
24→        sh.emit(1) // no suspend
25→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
26→        assertEquals(0, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
27→        expect(2)
28→        // one collector
29→        val job1 = launch(start = CoroutineStart.UNDISPATCHED) {
30→            expect(3)
31→            sh.collect {
32→                when(it) {
33→                    4 -> expect(5)
34→                    6 -> expect(7)
35→                    10 -> expect(11)
36→                    13 -> expect(14)
37→                    else -> expectUnreached()
38→                }
39→            }
40→            expectUnreached() // does not complete normally
41→        }
42→        expect(4)
43→        assertEquals(1, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
44→        sh.emit(4)
45→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
46→        expect(6)
47→        sh.emit(6)
48→        expect(8)
49→        // one more collector
50→        val job2 = launch(start = CoroutineStart.UNDISPATCHED) {
51→            expect(9)
52→            sh.collect {
53→                when(it) {
54→                    10 -> expect(12)
55→                    13 -> expect(15)
56→                    17 -> expect(18)
57→                    null -> expect(20)
58→                    21 -> expect(22)
59→                    else -> expectUnreached()
60→                }
61→            }
62→            expectUnreached() // does not complete normally
63→        }
64→        expect(10)
65→        assertEquals(2, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
66→        sh.emit(10) // to both collectors now!
67→        assertTrue(sh.replayCache.isEmpty())
68→        expect(13)
69→        sh.emit(13)
70→        expect(16)
71→        job1.cancel() // cancel the first collector
72→        yield()
73→        assertEquals(1, sh.subscriptionCount.value)
74→        expect(17)
75→        sh.emit(17) // only to second collector
76→        expect(19)
77→        sh.emit(null) // emit null to the second collector
78→        expect(21)
79→        sh.emit(21) // non-null again
80→        expect(23)
